description:
You are an integration developer who has just pulled yesterday’s mock-API artefacts onto the filesystem and must do a quick integrity check before pushing them into the CI pipeline.

        Files that already exist:
        1. /home/user/api_response.txt  
           • Plain-text, **no** trailing newline, exactly five ASCII characters:  
             hello
        2. /home/user/reference.sha256  
           • One line, classic “sha256sum” format:  
             <64-hex-digit-hash>␠␠api_response.txt  

        What you must do:
        A. Verify the SHA-256 checksum of /home/user/api_response.txt against the value stored in /home/user/reference.sha256.  
        B. Create the directory /home/user/api_test/logs/ (make any missing parent directories).  
        C. Inside that directory create a text file named integrity.log whose content **exactly** follows this three-line template (one line each, no extra spaces, no extra blank lines):  
           EXPECTED_SHA256=<hash_from_reference_file>  
           ACTUAL_SHA256=<hash_you_computed>  
           MATCH=<true_or_false>  

           • EXPECTED_SHA256 must be the 64-character hash string appearing in /home/user/reference.sha256.  
           • ACTUAL_SHA256 must be the 64-character hash string you calculate for /home/user/api_response.txt.  
           • MATCH must be the lowercase word “true” if the two hashes are identical, otherwise “false”.

        D. Leave the original files untouched.

        Notes & constraints:
        • Carry out the task entirely from the bash shell; 2-3 concise commands are enough.  
        • Do not edit /home/user/reference.sha256 or /home/user/api_response.txt.  
        • The automated grader will read /home/user/api_test/logs/integrity.log and expects it to match the exact format above, including letter case.  
        • You do not need root privileges for any step.
truth:
Initial files on disk BEFORE the agent starts:

        1. /home/user/api_response.txt  
           Content (5 bytes, no trailing newline):  
           hello

        2. /home/user/reference.sha256  
           Content (including trailing newline):  
           2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824  api_response.txt\n

        3. No /home/user/api_test/ directory yet.

        EXPECTED final artefact to be CREATED by the agent:

        • /home/user/api_test/logs/integrity.log  
          Expected exact content (including trailing newline after last line):  
          EXPECTED_SHA256=2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824  
          ACTUAL_SHA256=2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824  
          MATCH=true\n

        The grading script will:
        • Re-compute sha256sum api_response.txt and compare to the value in reference.sha256.  
        • Confirm that integrity.log exists, is readable, and the three lines match exactly the format above.  
        • Verify that MATCH=true and that the ACTUAL_SHA256 equals the EXPECTED_SHA256.
name: task_000000_cdca2549
